finding: Any-platform high unlock-fee penalty survives multiple high-fee cutoffs
evidence: For any platform membership, unlock_fee_ge_0_99, unlock_fee_ge_1_00, and unlock_fee_ge_1_20 are negative in 3/6 models each. Market-FE all estimates are -0.160 (p < 0.001), -0.158 (p < 0.001), and -0.155 (p < 0.001), respectively; no-weak market-FE estimates are -0.111, -0.110, and -0.108 (all p < 0.001).
interpretation: The high unlock-fee finding is not an artifact of one median split; the negative platform interaction remains when high fees are defined at EUR 0.99, EUR 1.00, or EUR 1.20.
caveat: The any-platform threshold effect is mainly market-FE, not unit-FE.

finding: Local MaaS high unlock-fee penalty is stable across all tested cutoffs
evidence: Local MaaS threshold interactions are negative in 5/6 models for every cutoff from any positive fee through EUR 1.20. At EUR 1.00: market FE all = -0.229 (p < 0.001), unit FE all = -0.049 (p = 0.002), no-weak market FE = -0.166 (p < 0.001), scooter market FE = -0.096 (p = 0.0035).
interpretation: The most defensible platform-type claim is that local MaaS contexts strongly amplify salient unlock-fee penalties.
caveat: Local MaaS is still partly city-specific; city-level heterogeneity should be acknowledged.

finding: Multi-platform high unlock-fee penalty is highly stable at EUR 1.00 and EUR 1.20
evidence: At EUR 1.00 and EUR 1.20, multi-platform interactions are negative in 6/6 models. EUR 1.00: market FE all = -0.146 (p < 0.001), unit FE all = -0.099 (p < 0.001). EUR 1.20: market FE all = -0.171 (p < 0.001), unit FE all = -0.082 (p < 0.001).
interpretation: Multi-platform exposure is a strong boundary condition for access-fee salience: high upfront fees are especially detrimental where users encounter multiple platform-mediated options.
caveat: Multi-platform observations are fewer than any-platform observations but still recur across samples and FE strategies.

finding: Free Now-only does not show a stable high unlock-fee threshold penalty
evidence: Large-aggregator-only interactions are not significant for any tested unlock-fee threshold; e.g., unlock_fee_ge_1_00:large_aggregator_only has market FE all = -0.045 (p = 0.066) and unit FE all = -0.016 (p = 0.325).
interpretation: This strengthens the earlier conclusion that the pricing-friction mechanism is not a Free Now-only large-aggregator result.
caveat: Free Now remains important as a broad platform source, but not as the strongest pricing-friction moderator.

finding: The any-platform high unlock-fee result is not fully driven by a single major operator
evidence: Leave-one-operator tests for unlock_fee_ge_0_99 remain negative and significant in market FE after dropping BOLT (-0.119, p < 0.001), NEXTBIKE (-0.052, p = 0.021), TIER (-0.091, p = 0.010), or VOI (-0.199, p < 0.001).
interpretation: The high-fee platform penalty is not only a single-operator artifact in market-FE specifications.
caveat: Leave-one-operator evidence is weaker in unit FE and some highest-threshold cells become sparse, especially after dropping TIER.
